title: What is Confidence Calibration?
concept: Confidence calibration ensures that a model's predicted probability matches its actual likelihood of being correct.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the benefit of hierarchical fallback when a model encounters a class it has never seen before?

options.text: It can safely predict a general parent category with high confidence.
It automatically labels the unseen class with 100% accuracy.
It makes the model run faster.

exercises.explain: Correct! Falling back to parent nodes prevents wild, incorrect guesses on unseen categories.

cards.title: The weather forecast
cards.body: If a weather forecaster says there is a 70% chance of rain, you expect it to rain on exactly 7 out of 10 days with that forecast. If it rains only 2 out of 10 times, the forecaster is poorly calibrated. Classification models face the same issue.

cards.title: What is Calibration?
cards.body: Confidence calibration is the degree to which a model's predicted probability (confidence) reflects its actual accuracy. A model is perfectly calibrated if, when predicting a class with 90% confidence, it is correct exactly 90% of the time.

cards.title: An honest friend vs. an exaggerator
cards.body: Think of calibration as honesty. A calibrated friend says 'I'm 99% sure' only when they are certain, and 'I'm 50% sure' when they are guessing. An uncalibrated friend claims to be '100% sure' about everything, even when they are just making wild guesses.

cards.title: Medical diagnostic scan
cards.body: A model flags an X-ray as having pneumonia with 98% confidence. If the hospital uses this score to bypass human review, but the model is uncalibrated and only correct 60% of the time on 98% predictions, patients are placed in severe danger.

cards.title: Accuracy is not calibration
cards.body: You might think a model with 95% accuracy must be well-calibrated. However, a model can have 95% accuracy but predict 100% confidence for every single image it processes, representing severe miscalibration.

left.text:
Predicts 'Dog' with 70% confidence
Over 100 similar predictions, 70 are correct
Confidence matches reality

right.text:
Predicts 'Dog' with 99% confidence
Over 100 similar predictions, only 70 are correct
Model exaggerates its certainty

exercises.prompt: When is a classification model considered perfectly calibrated?
exercises.explain: Calibration measures the alignment between the model's confidence scores and its true accuracy rates.

options.text: When its predicted confidence level matches its actual frequency of being correct
When it achieves 100% accuracy on the test set
When all logits are transformed into negative numbers
When it outputs identical probabilities for all classes

exercises.prompt: True or false: A model that has 90% accuracy but predicts 100% confidence for all correct and incorrect predictions is well-calibrated.
exercises.explain: False. Since its confidence (100%) does not match its accuracy (90%), it is miscalibrated (specifically, overconfident).
80% confidence means an 80% success rate, which is 8 out of 10 times.
exercises.sentence: If a calibrated model predicts a target with 80% confidence, it should be correct ___ out of 10 times.

exercises.prompt: Which of these outputs represents a calibrated prediction?
exercises.explain: A prediction is calibrated if the stated confidence rate aligns with the actual success rate.

options.text: Predicted: Cat (Confidence: 80%) where the model is correct 80% of the time on such predictions
Predicted: Cat (Confidence: 99%) where the model is correct 50% of the time on such predictions
Predicted: Cat (Confidence: 100%) on all validation images

exercises.prompt: You are building a self-driving car model to detect traffic lights. The system must hand control back to the driver if it is unsure of the light's color. Why is calibration critical here?

options.text: Because if the model is uncalibrated and overconfident, it might run a red light with 99% stated confidence while being wrong.
Because calibration makes the camera lens focus faster.
Because calibration reduces the car's electrical consumption.
Because uncalibrated models cannot detect colors at all.

exercises.explain: Exactly! If the model is overconfident, it will report 99% confidence even when it is highly uncertain, failing to hand control back to the human driver. Calibration ensures the system's safety-net functions correctly. Next, let's look at why modern models suffer from this issue!
